name: The Disputed Zone — Fringe
descriptionSeed: The band of broken country where Harmonic and Radiant power fields interfere. Sound bends light here; light carries sound. Standing waves make pockets where time runs syrup-slow, and shimmer-vortices wander like dust devils made of noise and glare. Neither civilization claims it — officially. Both send people into it. Things live here that adapted to the interference, and things were left here during the Great Transition that never got collected.
encounterFlavor: Interference phenomena, scavengers and smugglers, adapted fauna, half-buried pre-Transition machinery, and rarely — very rarely — something that predates humanity noticing you.
questSeeds: The survey-grid lights move in patterns — plotting them means camping in interference country for a night, which nobody has survived unchanged
Scavengers pay well for pre-Transition salvage but their broker asks oddly specific questions about anything with Precursor markings
A time-pocket has trapped a courier mid-stride for nine days; both civilizations claim jurisdiction and neither will act
Something in the Zone has started answering sonic pings with pings of its own — one tone off, like an echo learning

encounterSeeds.hint: Scavenger-raiders work the fringe — one may decide a traveler's pack is salvage

axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: Near-centre folk country — the Valley sits where all axes cross faintly, so longitude spreads and colatitude stays low. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
